hell_encounter_anger.title: The Angry Bully
hell_encounter_anger.description: You encounter someone spreading cruel rumors about you. Others are laughing. Your face burns with humiliation and anger rises in your chest. What will you do?

choices.text: I'll spread even worse rumors about them!

consequences.message: Revenge only creates more suffering. The poison of hatred burns in your own heart, causing you pain.

choices.text: This hurts, but revenge won't heal me.

consequences.message: You recognized the cycle of hatred. The pain remains, but you chose not to add fuel to the fire of anger.

choices.text: What pain are they carrying that makes them act this way?

consequences.message: Deep wisdom arises. Hurt people hurt people. You see the bully is also suffering, trapped in their own anger and pain.

hungry_ghost_craving.title: The Endless Want
hungry_ghost_craving.description: You find yourself constantly scrolling through social media, comparing yourself to others. Each post makes you want more - more possessions, more success, more validation. But nothing satisfies.

choices.text: Just one more scroll. Maybe the next post will make me feel better.

consequences.message: The craving grows stronger. No amount of scrolling fills the emptiness inside. You're trapped in endless wanting.

choices.text: I notice this feeling of 'not enough.' What am I really seeking?

consequences.message: You begin to see the nature of craving - it's not about the objects, but about an inner hunger that can't be filled externally.

choices.text: Put the phone down. Breathe. This moment is already enough.

consequences.message: A moment of peace. You realize contentment comes not from having more, but from wanting less. The hungry ghost loses its power.

animal_ignorance.title: Following the Herd
animal_ignorance.description: Your friends are pressuring you to do something you know is harmful - spreading gossip, cheating on a test, or excluding someone. Everyone else is doing it. If you don't, you'll be the outsider.

choices.text: Go along with it. I don't want to be left out.

consequences.message: Acting without thinking, like an animal following the herd. Your conscience troubles you, but you ignored its voice.
You avoided doing harm, but fear kept you silent. A small step toward awareness, but more courage is needed.

choices.text: Speak up: 'This doesn't feel right. I'm not doing this.'

consequences.message: True courage! You broke free from the herd and followed your conscience. Some may judge you, but you kept your integrity.

human_loss.title: The Grief of Loss
human_loss.description: Someone you deeply love has died. The grief feels unbearable. Everything reminds you of them. The world seems empty and meaningless without their presence.

choices.text: Pretend they're not really gone. Avoid thinking about it.

consequences.message: Denial postpones the pain but prevents healing. The grief remains, buried but not resolved, waiting to surface.

choices.text: Rage at the unfairness. Why did this happen?!

consequences.message: Anger is natural in grief, but clinging to it creates suffering. The universe doesn't operate on fairness - this is dukkha.

choices.text: Honor their memory while accepting impermanence. All meetings end in parting.

consequences.message: Profound understanding. Death is not a mistake but part of life's nature. Love transcends loss. Their impact on you remains, transformed but not destroyed.
